title: Search Keyword
gist: SNB corporate/shareholder landing page (the raw title 'Search Keyword' is a scraper artifact). It carries no monetary-policy thesis but does disclose the listed SNB share's capped 6%-of-nominal dividend mechanics, its bond-like price behaviour, the 2026 AGM decisions, and a multi-year taxable-value series for the share.

insights.claim: The Swiss National Bank is a listed joint-stock company with CHF 25 million of share capital split into 100,000 registered shares of CHF 250 nominal; ~55% is held by public shareholders (cantons, cantonal banks) and the Confederation holds none.
insights.speaker: SNB Research
insights.so_what: The SNB equity (security no. 131926) is an investable, listed instrument whose ownership is dominated by cantons, not the federal government.
insights.quote: The share capital amounts to CHF 25 million, approximately 55% of which is held by public shareholders (cantons, cantonal banks, etc.).
insights.locator: The SNB as a joint-stock company

insights.claim: By law the SNB share dividend cannot exceed 6% of nominal value, so its price tends to track a long-term Confederation bond carrying a 6% coupon; the 2026 dividend is CHF 15.00 gross (CHF 9.75 net) per share.
insights.speaker: SNB Research
insights.so_what: The SNB share trades as a quasi-fixed-income / rates proxy, not as a claim on central-bank profits or FX-reserve gains, so its valuation moves with long Swiss yields.
insights.quote: Due to the legally stipulated maximum dividend of 6%, the price of the SNB share usually develops along similar lines to a long-term Confederation bond with a 6% coupon.

insights.claim: The taxable value of the SNB share has compressed sharply, falling from CHF 5,390 (end-2019) to CHF 3,530 (end-2025) after a low of CHF 3,190 at end-2024.
insights.speaker: SNB Research
insights.so_what: The multi-year decline in the share's taxable value is consistent with the rates-proxy framing and reflects the higher-yield regime since 2022.

insights.claim: At the 24 April 2026 AGM, SNB shareholders approved the 2025 financial report and a total dividend of CHF 1.5 million, granted discharge to the Bank Council, elected Swissmem president Martin Hirzel to the Bank Council, and appointed KPMG Ltd as external auditor for 2026-2027.
insights.speaker: SNB Research
insights.so_what: Governance continuity at the SNB, with industry representation (Swissmem) added to the Bank Council; routine for shareholders tracking the listed share.
insights.locator: Decisions of the 2026 General Meeting of Shareholders
